plant.description: Unique History: Mentioned in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita as the best among 'Vata-pacifying' seeds. Historically used as a primary oil source for scholars and high-performance individuals due to its 'Ojas' enhancing quality. Regarded as 'Mother of Flow'. Physical Characteristics: A small, erect, annual herb reaching up to 0.5-1.5 meters in height, with quadrangular stems and opposite, pinnate (leaflets) leaves with small, white-to-yellowish flowers. The fruit consists of small follicles (seeds) containing numerous small, flat, greyish-white-to-brown seeds. Found throughout India and tropical regions worldwide, cultivated extensively for its seeds. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Siringic acid, Ferulic acid, and diversos Protease inhibitors. The presence of phenolic acids is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-tumor, and anti-inflammatory action on the heart and skin cells. Strict Standardizations: The seed is standardized in API based on its greyish-white-to-white color and characteristic sweet-mild-nutty taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<8%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>15%).

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Used in Vata-vyadhi (neuromuscular disorders), Kushtha (Skin diseases), Shotha (Edema), Krimi (Worms), and Artava-dosha (menstrual disorders).

kusumbha_taila__safflower_oil_.method: Used for local massage (Abhyanga) in joint pain and to treat hair issues (Keshya).

seed_powder__churna_.method: 1-3g of fine powder taken with warm water once daily for managing worms and digestion.

roasted_seeds__bhrista_.method: 30g of roasted seeds powder taken daily for Managing weight-gain and strength.

external_paste__lepa_.method: Ground fresh seeds or powder applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, joint pain, skin rashes) to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 125 mg to 250 mg of powder mixed with honey once twice for respiratory support during childhood infections.
dosage.adults: 1-3 grams of powder or 5-10 ml of oil divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.elderly: Highly recommended to prevent age-related cold issues and maintain circulatory health. Dosage: 1g of powder daily.

medicinal_uses: Expert Internal Lubrication and Vata Specialist (Snehana): Kusumbha is the premier Ayurvedic seed for managing chronic dryness and joint stiffness. Its 'Snigdha' (oily) and 'Ushna' (heating) nature provided by high Linoleic acid helps in provides systemic relief from 'Vata-avarana' (Vata-obstruction) in the pelvic region. WHO-aligned research confirms its potent cholesterol-lowering and heart-soothing properties.

Skin Disease and Blood-Purifying Specialist (Kusthaghna): Historically known as 'Raktaprasadana' (blood-soothing). It effectively reduces the intensity and frequency of localized inflammations and chronic skin issues by providing systemic 'Vranaropana' (wound-healing) and clearing toxic Pitta from the skin layers through its systemic 'Sara' (flowing) action.

Reproductive Health and Aphrodisiac (Vrishya): Associated with 'Vajikarana' (aphrodisiac). It improves sexual vigor, sperm count, and libido by providing systemic nourishing (Brimhana) action and improving blood circulation in the pelvic region.

Lactation support: Traditionally used to improve breast milk production (Stanya-janana) in lactating mothers by providing systemic nourishment and provides systemic hormonal balance through its 'Madhura' (sweet) and 'Ushna' (heating) properties.
Urinary and Kidney Support: As a mild diuretic, it helps in clearing urinary stagnations and managing infections by cooling the Pitta in the bladder and kidneys.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Proteins (16-18%), Oats (30-35%), Linoleic acid (75%), Phenolic acids, and Essential Minerals (Phosphorus, Magnesium, Calcium). The presence of phenolic acids is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-tumor, and anti-inflam-matory action on the heart and skin cells.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb and oil source with a history of documented safety for thousands of years.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Pitta (burning sensation, hyperacidity) should use it in moderation as it is Ushna (heating). Toxicity is extreme; non-purified use or overdose is lethal. Strictly avoid in pregnancy in very large therapeutic doses except under professional guidance (as it may stimulate contractions).
possible_side_effects: Overconsumption in sensitive individuals can cause Pitta aggravation and burning if not appropriately cooked.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside medications as it significantly increases the bioavailability of many drugs. Can potentiate synthetic anticoagulants.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
